title: cirsium occidentale var. coulteri
intro: Cirsium occidentale var. coulteri, commonly known as Coulter's thistle, is a variety of the Western thistle plant (Cirsium occidentale) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Coulter's thistle is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
